lit. one scale and half a claw (idiom)
fig. only odd bits and pieces
to amaze the world with a single brilliant feat (idiom); an overnight celebrity
(used in negative expressions) (not) the least bit
(after an adjective, used to form the comparative) a bit more, -er
one o'clock (abbr. for 一點鍾|一点钟[yi1 dian3 zhong1])
erhua variant of 一點|一点[yi1 dian3]
a hint is all that is needed
understanding each other without the need to explain

pinyin: yi1 dian3 shui3 yi1 ge4 pao4

defs: lit. to breathe through the same nostril (idiom); fig. two people say exactly the same thing (usually derog.)
to sing from the same hymn sheet
